0.original: Pat them on the back for the job they're doing.
0.b1: Praise them for the work they are doing.
0.ai_notes: 'Pat them on the back': a gesture of praise or encouragement. It means to congratulate someone for their hard work.
0.remarks: 'Pat them on the back': 轻拍背部，引申为对他人的努力表示赞赏或鼓励。

1.ai_notes: 'Give them hope': to make someone feel that something good will happen in the future.
1.remarks: 'Give them hope': 给予希望，指让某人对未来感到乐观。

2.original: Let them know you believe they can succeed, that you have faith in them.
2.b1: Tell them you believe they can succeed and that you trust them.
2.cn: 告诉他们你相信他们能成功，并且信任他们。
2.ai_notes: 'Have faith in': to trust someone or believe that they have the ability to do something well.
2.remarks: 'Have faith in': 对某人有信心，相信其具备完成任务的能力。

3.b1: Practice helping people who are worried.
3.cn: 练习去安抚那些忧虑的人。
3.ai_notes: 'Relieving': making someone feel less worried or stressed. 'Worriers': people who spend a lot of time thinking about problems.
3.remarks:
'Relieving': 令人宽慰的，减轻压力。
'Worriers': 爱担忧的人，指那些总是为问题烦恼的人。

4.original: Make this little test regularly to keep you on the right track.
4.b1: Do this simple test often to make sure you are on the right path.
4.cn: 经常做这个小测试，以确保你走在正确的道路上。
4.ai_notes: 'On the right track': moving in the right direction or doing something in a correct way.
4.remarks: 'On the right track': 在正确的轨道上，指进展方向正确。

5.original: Whenever you leave a person, ask yourself, does that person honestly feel better because he has talked with me?
5.b1: Whenever you finish talking to someone, ask yourself if they feel better after speaking with you.
5.cn: 每当你与人交谈结束时，问问自己，对方在与你交谈后是否真的感觉更好了？
5.ai_notes: 'Honestly': in a truthful or sincere way. This question is a way to check if your communication is positive.
5.remarks: 'Honestly': 诚实地，用于表达真诚的态度。

6.cn: 这种自我训练的方法很有效。
6.ai_notes: 'Self-training device': a tool or method you use to improve your own behavior or skills.
6.remarks: 'Self-training device': 自我训练工具，指用于改善个人行为或技能的方法。

7.original: Apply it when talking with employees, associates, your family, customers, even with casual acquaintances.
7.b1: Use it when talking to employees, friends, family, customers, or even strangers.
7.cn: 在与员工、同事、家人、客户甚至点头之交交谈时，都运用它。
7.ai_notes: 'Associates': people you work with. 'Casual acquaintances': people you know slightly but are not close friends with.
7.remarks:
'Associates': 同事或合作伙伴。
'Casual acquaintances': 泛泛之交，指认识但不亲密的人。

8.original: A salesman friend is a real good news broadcaster.
8.b1: A salesman friend of mine is very good at sharing good news.
8.cn: 我的一位推销员朋友非常擅长传播好消息。
8.ai_notes: 'News broadcaster': someone who shares information or news with others.
8.remarks: 'News broadcaster': 新闻广播员，指负责传播信息的人。

9.original: He calls on his customers every month and always makes it a rule to have some good news to pass along.
9.b1: He visits his customers every month and always makes sure to share some good news.
9.cn: 他每个月都会拜访客户，并且总是习惯带去一些好消息分享。
9.ai_notes: 'Calls on': visits someone, often for business purposes. 'Make it a rule': to decide to always do something as a habit.
9.remarks:
'Calls on': 拜访，常用于商务场合。
'Make it a rule': 养成习惯，指决定将某事作为固定准则。

10.original: Examples I met one of your good friends last week.
10.b1: For example: I met one of your good friends last week.
10.cn: 例如：我上周见到了你的一位好朋友。
10.ai_notes: 'Examples': a way to show or explain something by giving a specific instance.
10.remarks: 'Examples': 例子，通过具体事例来解释说明。

11.original: He said to tell you hello.
11.b1: He asked me to say hello to you.
11.ai_notes: 'Tell you hello': a common way to say that someone sent their best wishes to you.
11.remarks: 'Tell you hello': 代某人问好，表达对他人的祝福。

12.original: Since I was here last, big things have happened.
12.b1: Since I was here last time, many big things have happened.
12.cn: 自从我上次来这里之后，发生了很多大事。
12.ai_notes: 'Since I was here last': a phrase used to refer to the time between a previous visit and the current moment.
12.remarks: 'Since I was here last': 自从我上次来这里以来，用于连接两次访问之间的时间。

13.original: Over 350,000 new babies were born last month, and more babies means more business for both of us.
13.b1: Over 350,000 babies were born last month, and more babies means more business for both of us.
13.cn: 上个月有超过35万名婴儿出生，更多的婴儿意味着我们双方都有更多的生意。
13.ai_notes: 'Pass along': to give information or news to someone else.
13.remarks: 'Pass along': 传递，指将信息或消息转告给他人。

14.original: Usually, we think of bank presidents as overly reserved, unemotional people who never really warm up.
14.b1: We usually think bank presidents are very serious and cold people.
14.cn: 我们通常认为银行行长是非常严肃、冷漠、不苟言笑的人。
14.ai_notes: 'Overly reserved': too quiet or serious. 'Warm up': to become more friendly or relaxed in social situations.
14.remarks:
'Overly reserved': 过分矜持，指性格过于安静或严肃。
'Warm up': 热络起来，指在社交中变得友好。

15.original: Not so with one bank president.
15.b1: But one bank president is different.
15.cn: 但有一位银行行长却不是这样。
15.ai_notes: 'Not so with': a phrase used to show that a specific person or situation is different from the general rule.
15.remarks: 'Not so with': 情况并非如此，用于指出某人或某事与众不同。

16.original: His favorite way to answer the phone is to say, Good morning, it's a wonderful world.
16.b1: He likes to answer the phone by saying, Good morning, it is a wonderful world.
16.cn: 他接电话时最喜欢说：“早上好，这是一个美好的世界。”
16.ai_notes: 'Wonderful world': a positive way to describe life, often used to show optimism.
16.remarks: 'Wonderful world': 美好的世界，常用于表达乐观的生活态度。

17.original: May I sell you some money?
17.b1: May I sell you some money?
17.ai_notes: 'Sell you some money': a humorous way for a banker to offer loans or financial services.
17.remarks: 'Sell you some money': 卖给你钱，银行家幽默的说法，指提供贷款服务。

18.b1: Is that strange for a banker?
18.cn: 这对银行家来说不合适吗？
18.ai_notes: 'Improper': not suitable or not following the expected rules of behavior.
18.remarks: 'Improper': 不恰当的，指不符合行为规范。

19.ai_notes: 'Some might say so': a way to acknowledge that other people may have a different opinion.
19.remarks: 'Some might say so': 有人可能会这么说，用于承认他人持有不同意见。

20.original: But let me point out that the banker who uses this greeting is Mills Lane,
20.b1: But this banker is Mills Lane,
20.cn: 但这位银行家是米尔斯·莱恩，
20.ai_notes: 'Point out': to draw attention to a specific fact or detail.
20.remarks: 'Point out': 指出，引起对某事实或细节的注意。

21.original: Jr., president of the Citizens and Southern Bank, the largest in the entire Southeast.
21.b1: the president of the largest bank in the Southeast.
21.cn: 他是东南部最大银行——公民南方银行的行长。
21.ai_notes: 'Southeast': a specific region in the United States, often referring to the states below the Mason-Dixon line.
21.remarks: 'Southeast': 美国东南部地区，指特定地理区域。

22.cn: 好消息会带来好的结果。
22.ai_notes: 'Good results': positive outcomes or successful consequences.
22.remarks: 'Good results': 好结果，指积极的成效。

23.ai_notes: 'Broadcast it': to share information widely or tell many people about it.
23.remarks: 'Broadcast it': 广播它，指广泛传播信息。

24.original: The president of a brush manufacturing company I visited recently had this maxim neatly framed on his desk facing the visitor's chair.
24.b1: The president of a brush company I visited recently had this saying framed on his desk.
24.cn: 我最近拜访的一家刷子制造公司的总裁，在办公桌上放了一个装裱好的座右铭。
24.ai_notes: 'Maxim': a short, well-known statement that expresses a general truth or rule of conduct. 'Framed': put inside a decorative border.
24.remarks:
'Maxim': 格言，表达真理或行为准则的短句。
'Framed': 装裱，指放入装饰性的框中。

25.original: Give me a good word or none at all.
25.b1: Say something good or say nothing at all.
25.cn: “要么说好话，要么什么也别说。”
25.ai_notes: 'None at all': not any; zero. This means if you cannot say something kind, it is better to stay silent.
25.remarks: 'None at all': 一点也没有，表示如果说不出好话，最好保持沉默。

26.original: I complimented him, saying that I thought the maxim was a clever way to encourage people to be optimistic.
26.b1: I told him that I thought it was a smart way to encourage optimism.
26.cn: 我称赞他，说我认为这是一种鼓励人们保持乐观的聪明方式。
26.ai_notes: 'Complimented': told someone something nice about them. 'Optimistic': feeling hopeful and confident about the future.
26.remarks:
'Complimented': 称赞，对某人说好话。
'Optimistic': 乐观的，对未来充满信心。

27.original: He smiled and said, it is an effective reminder, but from where I sit, this is even more important.
27.b1: He smiled and said it is helpful, but he thinks something else is more important.
27.cn: 他微笑着说，这确实是一个有效的提醒，但从我的角度来看，还有更重要的。
27.ai_notes: 'From where I sit': a way to say 'in my opinion' or 'from my perspective'.
27.remarks: 'From where I sit': 在我看来，用于表达个人观点。

28.original: He turned the frame around so I could see it from his side of the desk.
28.b1: He turned the frame around so I could see the other side.
28.cn: 他把相框转过来，让我能从他那一侧看到内容。
28.ai_notes: 'Turn the frame around': to rotate an object so that the other side is visible.
28.remarks: 'Turn the frame around': 把框转过来，指旋转物体以展示另一面。

29.original: It said, give them a good word or none at all.
29.b1: It said, give them a good word or none at all.
29.cn: 上面写着：“给他们一句好话，或者什么也别说。”
29.ai_notes: 'Give them a good word': to say something positive or encouraging to another person.
29.remarks: 'Give them a good word': 为某人美言几句，指给予积极的评价。

30.original: Broadcasting good news activates you, makes you feel better.
30.b1: Sharing good news makes you more active and feel better.
30.cn: 传播好消息会让你变得积极，让你感觉更好。
30.ai_notes: 'Activates': makes you feel more energetic or ready to take action.
30.remarks: 'Activates': 激活，指让人感到更有活力并采取行动。

31.original: Broadcasting good news makes other people feel better too.
31.b1: Sharing good news makes other people feel better too.
31.cn: 传播好消息也会让别人感觉更好。
31.ai_notes: 'Broadcasting': in this context, it means spreading positive energy or news to others.
31.remarks: 'Broadcasting': 广播，在此语境下指传播正能量或消息。
